concepts.definition_en: critera based on definitions of bauxite. Thoenen and Burchard (1941, p.38), modfied by Denson and Waage (166, p.6) place boundary between bauxitic clay and bauxite at 45 percent alumina (Al2O3). (Quoted from Mallick, K.A., 1970, PhD, University of Karachi)
note overlap of this category with QAPF9_10 (among other possible QAPF overlaps)

concepts.label_pt: tefra de cinza, brecha, bomba e bloco
tufo de cinza, lapilito e tufo lapílico

concepts.definition_en: EarthChem (12/28/09, http://earthchem.org) reports 119109 analyses reported as basalt by the collector. Of these 76937 have Silica between 45 and 52 percent. Thus ChemCat is basic.

EarthChem (12/28/09, http://earthchem.org) reports 3041 analyses for rocks called basanite or basanitoid by the collector. In TAS plot (2095 samples), most of these lie between 41 and 52 percent silica, thus basic or ultrabasic ChemCat

Note that for sedimentary genetic categories, the genesis refers to the genetic origin of particles in a sedimentary rock. Because a given sediment may mix particles of different origin (pyroclastic, epiclastic, chemical, biogenic), the sedimentary genetic categories should be understood to mean that 'most of the particles in the sedimentary rock have this genesis'

Note that for sedimentary genetic categories, the genesis refers to the genetic origin of particles in a sedimentary rock. Because a given sediment may mix particles of different origin (pyroclastic, epiclastic, chemical, biogenic), the sedimentary genetic categories should be understood to mean that 'most of the particles in the sedimentary rock have this genesis'

Note that for sedimentary genetic categories, the genesis refers to the genetic origin of particles in a sedimentary rock. Because a given sediment may mix particles of different origin (pyroclastic, epiclastic, chemical, biogenic), the sedimentary genetic categories should be understood to mean that 'most of the particles in the sedimentary rock have this genesis'
technically not quite disjoint with sand_size. A rock might contain 27 percent fragments greater than 64 mm, and the rest be less than 2 mm...
EarthChem (12/28/09, http://earthchem.org) reports 13257 analyses for samples called dacite by collector. Of these, 8215 analyses have greater than 63 percent silica reported. Thus ChemCat is acidic

EarthChem (12/28/09, http://earthchem.org) reports 7928 analyses for material called diorite by collector. Of the 5227 with silica analytical results, 905 are basic, 3053 are intemediate, and 1163 are acidic (breaks 45, 52, 63). Intermediate is a weak correlation, especially for quartz diorite.

concepts.label_en: Phaneritic crystalline rock with M less than 90, consisting of intermediate plagioclase, commonly with hornblende and often with biotite or augite. A dioritoid with a plagioclase to total feldspar ratio (in the QAPF fraction) greater than 0.9.  Includes rocks defined modally in QAPF fields 10, 10' and 10*.

concepts.definition_en: Note that if TAS classification is used, the restriction to fine-grained for GrainSize has to be modified to 'not (phaneritic or gravel_size) to allow for glassy or aphanitic rocks that are categorized based on chemical analysis.
EarthChem (12/28/09, http://earthchem.org) reports 91 analyses for rock called foidite by collector; of these 71 contain less than 52 percent silica; included foidite as basis or ultrabasic ChemCat
not disjoint with M_LT90 categories because glassy igneous rocks may be given fine-grained igneous rock names based on phenocryst assemblage or chemical analysis.

concepts.label_en: defined only based on gravel-size grain size;  constituent type or sedimentary genesis not specified

concepts.definition_en: grain size is fine grained or phaneritic because komatiitic rocks are commonly too coarse grained to meet the definition of fine-grained
concepts.label_pt: rocha ígnea de granulação fina rica em magnésio

concepts.definition_en: Note that for sedimentary genetic categories, the genesis refers to the genetic origin of particles in a sedimentary rock. Because a given sediment may mix particles of different origin (pyroclastic, epiclastic, chemical, biogenic), the sedimentary genetic categories should be understood to mean that 'most of the particles in the sedimentary rock have this genesis'
Igneous rock containing greater than 10 percent melilite or kalsilite. Typically undersaturated, ultrapotassic (kalsilitic rocks) or calcium-rich (melilitic rocks) mafic or ultramafic rocks.
EarthChem (12/28/09, http://earthchem.org) reports 429 analyses for monzodiorite. 289 of these have silica results, and these scatter between 52 and 70 percent. Broadly intermediate in ChemCat.
Consists of greater than 50 percent organic rich solids (macerals), e.g. that make coal.

concepts.label_pt: material sedimentar rico em matéria orgânica
rocha sedimentar rica em matéria orgânica

concepts.definition_en: almost disjoint with sand_size. Phaneritic is generally used to describe igneous and metamorphic rocks, and the mud, sand, gravel spectrum for sedimentary rocks.

EarthChem (12/28/09, http://earthchem.org) reports 2771 analyses from rocks called phonolite by the collector.  Of these a vast majority lie between 46 and 53 percent silica in TAS plot (showing 1676 of the analyses). 1464 analyses have between 52 and 63 percent silica, and 539 between 45 and 52 percent silica.

concepts.label_pt: rocha ígnea fanerítica rica em quartzo

concepts.definition_en: The sand size range overlaps the fine-grained to phaneritic size range as commonly used in description of igneous or metamorphic rocks

Note that for sedimentary genetic categories, the genesis refers to the genetic origin of particles in a sedimentary rock. Because a given sediment may mix particles of different origin (pyroclastic, epiclastic, chemical, biogenic), the sedimentary genetic categories should be understood to mean that 'most of the particles in the sedimentary rock have this genesis'
include particleType noParticles because some coal and non-clastic silica may be amorphous

EarthChem (12/28/09, http://earthchem.org) reports 685 analyses for rocks called tephrite by the collector. Most of these plot between 42 and 54 percent silica (446 analyses in plot). 185 analyses have less than 45 percent silica; 413 have between 45 and 52 percent silica. consider basic to ultrabasic
Note that based on analyitcal data reported by EarthChem (12/28/09), most of the rock types generally considered ultraBasic have reported silica contents between 52 and about 40 percent...
